summary: Implemented CalendarComputer service with computeMonth, computeDay, getTodayPlan, skipTrainingDay, unskipTrainingDay, getSkippedDates, and getConsecutiveSkips methods. Supports weekly_fixed (weekday-to-training-day mapping) and fixed_interval (cycling through training days with configurable rest gaps) scheduling modes. Skipped dates persisted as JSON in user_settings. Day type resolution prioritizes: workout session > skipped > other sport > training > rest.
keyDecisions: 2.2: CalendarComputer interface updated to include planBizKey parameter on skipTrainingDay/unskipTrainingDay (skipped dates are per-plan, stored as skipped_dates_{planBizKey} in user_settings)
2.2: fixed_interval mode uses deterministic day-1-based cycle computation (no dependency on plan start date)
2.2: CalendarComputerServiceImpl typed as alias of CalendarComputer (no empty interface)
2.2: Day type priority: completed session > skipped > other_sport (on rest day) > training > other_sport (on training day) > rest
2.2: Completed workout session overrides skip status (user can unskip by doing the workout)
2.2: Added findByDateRange to OtherSportRepo for batch-loading sport records in computeMonth

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: computeMonth(year, month, plan, days) returns CalendarDay[] for all days in the month
weekly_fixed mode: maps weekday -> training_day_biz_key from plan.weekly_config JSON
fixed_interval mode: cycles through training days with rest_days gaps between
Past dates with WorkoutSession show actual status (completed/completed_partial)
Past dates without session but plan has training -> dayType='training' (user may have skipped)
skipTrainingDay(date) stores skipped date in user_settings JSON array
unskipTrainingDay(date) removes from skipped list and restores assignment
getConsecutiveSkips(planBizKey, beforeDate) returns consecutive skip count
Other sport records displayed on correct dates
Unit tests: weekly_fixed and fixed_interval scheduling, skip/unskip, consecutive skips
